project.name: SearXNG Docker Tavily Adapter
project.description: A free, self-hosted Tavily API replacement powered by SearXNG that provides Tavily-compatible search API with zero cost, no limits, and full privacy

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Environment configuration file for local development settings and secrets.

nodes.name: CONFIG_SETUP.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive setup guide covering unified YAML configuration for both SearXNG and Tavily Adapter with deployment instructions.

nodes.name: Caddyfile
nodes.summary: Caddy reverse proxy configuration with reverse proxy to SearXNG, comprehensive security headers, CSP, and API routing.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation with architecture overview, API usage examples, development flow, and mermaid diagrams illustrating request flows.

nodes.name: config.example.yaml
nodes.summary: Unified YAML configuration template for both SearXNG backend and Tavily Adapter frontend with search engine and scraper settings.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yaml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration for Redis/Valkey cache, SearXNG search engine, and Tavily Adapter API service deployment.

nodes.name: searxng-docker.service.template
nodes.summary: Systemd service template for running SearXNG outside Docker with environment variable substitution support.

nodes.name: limiter.toml
nodes.summary: SearXNG bot detection and rate limiting configuration with IP-based protection settings.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for Tavily Adapter Python application with FastAPI and aiohttp dependencies.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependencies for Tavily Adapter including FastAPI, aiohttp, beautifulsoup4, and pydantic.

nodes.name: config_loader.py
nodes.summary: Centralized configuration management using Python Config class with YAML parsing and property-based accessors for SearXNG adapter settings.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI server implementation providing Tavily-compatible /search endpoint that forwards requests to SearXNG with optional raw content scraping.

nodes.name: tavily_client.py
nodes.summary: Tavily-compatible Python client library with async search methods and Pydantic models for request/response serialization.

nodes.name: test_client.py
nodes.summary: Test client script for verifying Tavily Client search functionality and response format compatibility.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: FastAPI server implementation providing Tavily-compatible endpoints

layers.name: Client Library Layer
layers.description: Python client library for Tavily-compatible API interaction

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Business logic, orchestration, and client library for backend services

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Containerization, reverse proxy, and deployment configuration

tour.title: Start Here: Understand the Big Picture
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project vision - a free Tavily API replacement powered by SearXNG with zero cost, no limits, and full privacy.

tour.title: Configuration: The Glue That Holds It All Together
tour.description: Explore CONFIG_SETUP.md and config.example.yaml to understand the unified configuration approach for both SearXNG and the Tavily Adapter.

tour.title: Infrastructure: Docker Compose Orchestration
tour.description: Understand the three-container architecture (redis cache, searxng search engine, tavily-adapter API) orchestrated by docker-compose.yaml.

tour.title: Dockerfile: How the Adapter is Built
tour.description: See how the Tavily Adapter is containerized using Python 3.11-slim with FastAPI, uvicorn, aiohttp, pydantic, and beautifulsoup4.

tour.title: Configuration Loading: Python Singleton Pattern
tour.description: Learn how config_loader.py uses a singleton Config class to provide centralized configuration access via config_loader.config.

tour.title: The FastAPI Server: Request Handling
tour.description: Trace the request flow in main.py - from receiving POST /search requests to calling SearXNG and returning Tavily-compatible responses.

tour.title: The Client Library: Drop-in Tavily Replacement
tour.description: Understand tavily_client.py which provides the same interface as the official tavily-python package, letting you just change the base_url.

tour.title: Testing: Verifying Compatibility
tour.description: See how test_client.py verifies the adapter produces Tavily-compatible responses with proper structure and raw content fields.

tour.title: End-to-End: Running the Full Stack
tour.description: Learn the complete workflow: clone, configure, docker compose up, and test with curl or the Python client.

tour.title: Deep Dive: The Raw Content Scraping Flow
tour.description: Explore what happens when include_raw_content=True - parallel HTML fetching, BeautifulSoup cleaning, and text extraction up to 2500 characters.

tour.title: Architecture Patterns You Can Reuse
tour.description: Identify reusable design patterns: API adapter pattern, singleton configuration, async client layer, and unified configuration.

tour.title: Deployment: Beyond Docker Compose
tour.description: Explore production deployment options including systemd service templates and Caddy reverse proxy with security headers.
